macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Wall Street Closes Fifth Straight Losing Week Amid Iran War Tensions
items.summary: The S&P 500 fell 1.7% to 6,368.85, Dow dropped 793 points (1.7%) to 45,166.64, and Nasdaq sank 2.1% to 20,948.36, marking the worst week since the Iran war began. Big Tech like Amazon (-4%), Meta (-4%), and Nvidia (-2.2%) weighed heavily, alongside consumer stocks such as Norwegian Cruise Line (-6.9%) and Starbucks (-4.8%).

items.title: President Trump Extends Deadline on Iran Power Plants
items.summary: Trump extended a deadline to 'obliterate' Iran's power plants to April 6 if Iran does not allow oil tankers to exit the Persian Gulf through the Strait of Hormuz. This followed market volatility tied to war hopes and oil disruptions.

items.title: Global Markets React to Oil Shock and Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: Rising oil prices from geopolitical tensions caused market volatility across Asia, Europe, and the U.S., with stocks falling and investor caution increasing due to higher transportation costs, manufacturing expenses, and inflation pressures.

items.title: Indian Markets Decline Sharply with Foreign Investor Exodus
items.summary: Sensex and Nifty indices dropped significantly as foreign investors pulled out tens of thousands of crores from Indian markets in March amid oil price surges and global volatility.

items.title: Major Index Rebalancing Impacts Institutional Flows
items.summary: Changes in major indices led to increased weights for some companies and reductions for others, triggering automatic money flows in or out of stocks by large institutional funds tracking these indices.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto Market Crashes: Bitcoin, XRP, Ethereum, Solana Down 6-8% Weekly Amid ETF Outflows and Options Expiry
items.summary: Major cryptocurrencies including Bitcoin, XRP, Ethereum, and Solana experienced a 6-8% weekly drop driven by a $14.16 billion Bitcoin options expiry on Deribit, simultaneous ETF outflows for BTC, ETH, and SOL on March 26, and macroeconomic pressures like rising oil prices and Fed inflation forecasts. Bitcoin fell to $65,720 with over $451 million in liquidations.

items.title: Clarity Act 2026 Divides Crypto Industry Over Stablecoin Yield Ban
items.summary: The Clarity Act's proposed ban on stablecoin yields has split the crypto sector, with Coinbase opposing it due to impacts on 19% of its Q3 2025 revenue, while Tether and DeFi-native teams support or are unaffected by the activity-based yield carveout. Lawmakers face pressure as the bill's timeline tightens.

items.title: Goldman Sachs Flags Crypto Market Bottom After 46% Decline in Linked Equities
items.summary: Goldman Sachs research on March 26 suggests the crypto downturn may be nearing a cyclical low, with Bitcoin consolidating between $60,000-$75,000 and crypto-linked equities stabilizing after a 46% drop from October 2025 peaks. The firm recommends buys like Coinbase and Robinhood.

items.title: Bitcoin Hits $126K ATH in Late 2025 But Now Tests $60K-$75K Range in Macro Capitulation
items.summary: Bitcoin, Ethereum, BNB, and Solana navigate a macro-driven pullback, with BTC range-bound at $60K-$75K post-$14B options expiry, ETH at $1,990 with record-low exchange reserves and 33% staked supply ahead of Glamsterdam upgrade, and BNB battling $90 support.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: OpenAI Prepares for Potential 2026 IPO Amid Sora Shutdown
items.summary: OpenAI is transforming a 230-year-old company and gearing up for what could be the biggest tech IPO of 2026, while unexpectedly shutting down its Sora video generation tool to prioritize core language models. The move coincides with Meta facing legal challenges and industry shifts like SK hynix's U.S. listing and Anthropic's legal win against the Trump administration.

items.title: Google Research: AI Intelligence Emerges from Simulated Multi-Agent Interactions
items.summary: Google researchers reveal that advanced reasoning models like DeepSeek-R1 and QwQ-32B develop intelligence through internal 'societies of thought' involving debates and multi-perspective interactions, challenging the idea of singular AI minds. This emergent social reasoning process boosts accuracy on complex tasks via reinforcement learning.

items.title: AI Coding Boom Challenges Application Security Teams
items.summary: The surge in AI-generated code is outpacing security reviews, shifting risks from individual lines to entire systems as development accelerates.

items.title: AutoML Techniques Drive AI Innovation in 2026
items.summary: Six cutting-edge AutoML techniques are automating model development, optimization, and scaling, with the market projected to reach USD 6.81 billion amid integration with generative AI for faster decision-making across industries.

items.title: Investors Doubt Cybersecurity Growth Amid AI Expansion
items.summary: Despite AI's rise, investors are questioning cybersecurity firms' growth prospects as the technology fails to spur significant budget increases.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Crimson Desert Faces AI Asset Controversy After 2M Sales
items.summary: Crimson Desert sold 2 million copies but drew backlash for undisclosed AI-generated assets, prompting developers to admit the issue, audit content, and plan replacements via patches while improving transparency.

items.title: GameStop Stock Downgraded to 'Sell' Amid Declining Sales
items.summary: Wall Street Zen downgraded GameStop from 'hold' to 'sell' due to a 27% revenue drop, 35% earnings decline, and struggles adapting to digital downloads and online retail shifts.

items.title: Nintendo Reportedly Planning New Starfox Game and Zelda Remake for Switch 2
items.summary: Industry insider NatetheHate claims Nintendo is developing a new Starfox title and Zelda remake targeted for Nintendo Switch 2 release in 2026.

items.title: Mega Cat Studios Releases God of War: Sons of Sparta
items.summary: Pittsburgh-based Mega Cat Studios launched God of War: Sons of Sparta, a 2D Metroidvania-style prequel to the franchise, digitally on PS5 as part of the series' 20th anniversary, developed by a 400-person global team.

items.title: YouTuber Votesaxon07 Found Guilty in Livestream Lie Case
items.summary: YouTube gamer Votesaxon07 was found guilty in a case involving a 'livestream lie,' as covered in FOX on Games industry analysis.

items.title: New Turn-Based RPG Fix-a-Bug Launches Today
items.summary: Fix-a-Bug, a pixelated roguelite RPG with procedurally generated mazes, traps, puzzles, and turn-based combat, is now available as a new release on March 28, 2026.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.
